newsSinceJun6: Take-private endgame: Francisco Partners is acquiring Blackline for C$9.00 cash per share plus a CVR worth up to C$0.50 contingent on an ARR target in fiscal 2027 (~C$850M fully diluted). Proxy voting deadline is June 11, 2026 and the special shareholder vote is June 15. ISS and Glass Lewis both recommended voting FOR (June 4 release), though Globe and Mail reports some shareholder opposition to the price. Stock at C$9.02 is pinned to the cash consideration; shares will be delisted from the TSX on closing. No new operational/AI-chain news; fiscal Q2 results are subsumed by the deal.

sources.label: Blackline PR - ISS and Glass Lewis recommend FOR the Francisco Partners arrangement (Jun 4, 2026)
Globe and Mail - Blackline Safety's $850-million privatization deal faces shareholder opposition
Investing.com - Francisco Partners to acquire Blackline Safety for up to $9.50/share
Fire & Safety Journal Americas - Blackline transitions to private ownership / vote details

verdicts.rankRationale: On a latent-alpha list this thesis is terminally capped: the stock trades at C$9.02 versus C$9.00 cash consideration with the vote on June 15 and proxy advisors recommending FOR, so the only remaining upside is the C$0.50 CVR stub and the name delists on close. Yesterday's hold did not reflect the take-private, so the imminent vote is decision-relevant new information warranting a decisive move down.
verdicts.bull: Deal breaks or a bump emerges from the reported shareholder opposition, briefly reopening the connected-safety optionality; CVR pays the full C$0.50 if the fiscal-2027 ARR target is hit, ~5% incremental.
verdicts.neutral: Deal closes as expected after the June 15 vote at C$9.00 cash plus CVR; stock is pinned, behaves as short-dated merger arb with negligible volatility, then leaves the investable universe.
verdicts.bear: Vote passes and the name delists with zero residual latent-AI alpha captured; holders who chase here lock in arb-level returns at best, and a deal break without a higher bid would mean a sharp drop back toward pre-deal levels.
verdicts.primaryCatalyst: June 15, 2026 special shareholder vote (proxy deadline June 11) and subsequent deal closing/TSX delisting.
verdicts.primaryRisk: Deal completes and the position becomes uninvestable for the list; conversely, an unlikely vote failure would gap the stock down before any standalone thesis could re-rate.
